title: Le président gagne plus de pouvoir sur les agences
gist: Some U.S. agencies, like the central bank (the Fed), are meant to be independent of the president. The Supreme Court blocked President Trump from immediately firing Fed governor Lisa Cook — but in a linked ruling it gave presidents much more power to remove the heads of other independent agencies. For companies that means the regulatory rules may now swing with each new president.

bd.meaning: In the United States, some agencies are independent.
chunks: in the United States (aux = à + les)
are independent — indépendantes takes -es to match agences (feminine plural)
bd.key: Adjective agrees with its noun. agences is feminine plural, so indépendantes ends in -es.

body.fr: Le président ne {{contrôle|controls}} pas ces agences.

bd.meaning: The president doesn't control these agencies.
chunks: doesn't control — the ne…pas sandwich around contrôle
bd.key: Negation sandwich: ne … pas. It wraps the verb: ne contrôle pas = 'does not control'.

body.fr: La {{banque centrale|central bank (the Fed)}} est une agence indépendante.

bd.meaning: The central bank is an independent agency.
bd.key: Adjective after the noun. indépendante follows agence and agrees with it (feminine singular).

body.fr: Le président Trump veut {{renvoyer|dismiss / fire}} Lisa Cook.

bd.meaning: President Trump wants to fire Lisa Cook.
chunks: to fire Lisa Cook — renvoyer stays plain after veut
bd.key: Helper + plain verb. veut (wants) changes with the subject; the next verb renvoyer stays plain.

bd.meaning: She works at the central bank.
bd.key: à = 'at / to'. à la banque = 'at the bank'. (With le it becomes au; with la it stays à la.)
dire = 'to say'. dit = 'says' — a top-frequency verb; its past a dit ('said') turns up further down.

body.fr: Pour le moment, Trump ne peut pas renvoyer Lisa Cook.

bd.meaning: For now, Trump cannot fire Lisa Cook.
chunks: Trump cannot fire — ne…pas wraps peut; renvoyer stays plain
bd.key: Negation + helper together. ne peut pas = 'cannot', and the plain verb still follows: ne peut pas renvoyer.

body.fr: Mais la Cour {{prend une autre décision|makes another decision}}.

bd.meaning: But the Court makes another decision.
chunks: makes another decision (prendre une décision = to make a decision)
bd.key: prendre une décision = 'to make a decision'. French prend ('takes') a decision where English 'makes' one — learn it as a set phrase.

body.fr: Maintenant, le président a {{plus de pouvoir|more power}}.

bd.meaning: Now, the president has more power.
bd.key: plus de = 'more'. plus de pouvoir = 'more power' — the mirror of moins de ('less').

body.fr: Il peut renvoyer les {{chefs|heads / bosses}} d'autres agences.

bd.meaning: He can fire the heads of other agencies.
chunks: the heads of other agencies (de → d' before a vowel)
bd.key: Helper + plain verb, again. peut (can) carries the subject; renvoyer stays plain.

chunks: it is / that's — the set phrase c'est
a big change — grand comes BEFORE the noun
bd.key: A few adjectives go BEFORE the noun. grand is one: un grand changement = 'a big change'.

bd.meaning: Before, the law protected these people.
chunks: the law protected (a protégé = past of protéger)
bd.key: Past = avoir + verb. a protégé = 'protected': a (avoir) + the past form protégé. Your 'past = avoir/être + verb' key.
c'est = 'it is / that's'. The everyday set phrase: c'est un problème = 'it's a problem'.

bd.meaning: The rules can change with each president.
chunks: the rules can change (pouvoir + plain verb)
bd.key: chaque = 'each / every'. It stays singular: chaque président = 'each president'.

body.fr: Trump dit : « Ce n'est pas fini. »

chunks: it's not over / finished — the ne…pas sandwich around est
bd.key: ne … pas around c'est. c'est becomes ce n'est pas: 'it is' → 'it is not'.

grammar.fr: la loi a protégé ces personnes
grammar.en: avoir + past participle = the past (passé composé). a (from avoir) + a past form protégé. Your 'past = avoir/être + verb' key.

grammar.fr: veut renvoyer / peut renvoyer / peuvent changer
grammar.en: helper + plain verb. veut, peut, peuvent carry the subject; the next verb stays plain. Your pouvoir / vouloir / devoir family.

grammar.fr: ne contrôle pas / ne peut pas / ce n'est pas
grammar.en: the ne … pas sandwich. It wraps the verb every time: ne contrôle pas, ne peut pas, ce n'est pas.

quiz.q: Qu'est-ce que la Cour suprême décide pour Lisa Cook ?
quiz.a: Pour le moment, Trump ne peut pas la renvoyer. (For now, Trump cannot fire her.)

flag: A legal / political story compressed to A1 and kept strictly neutral — a factual retelling of your article, not a view on the ruling or the people. I dropped the detail: the F.T.C. commissioner's parallel case, the 5–4 split, the mortgage-fraud allegations, and the new Fed chair. Two word choices: la banque centrale stands in for 'the Fed', and renvoyer ('to send away') is the everyday verb for 'fire' — for removing an official a lawyer might use révoquer. agence is a fair plain word for these regulators, though the source is more specific.
